titleStmt.title: SII 19.28: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 28. (A.R. No. 192 of 1927.) KĀVĒRIPURAM, BHAVANI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT. ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE JALAKAṆṬHĒŚVARA TEMPLE.

summary.p: This records an endowment in gold of 4 kaḻañju per year, a piece of wet land, a house and a garden, made by the Vaḷañjiyar (community) of Tuvaḷai, as jīvita for Araiyaṉ Araiyaṉ and his heirs, for playing on the drum during the śrībali service in the temple of Nāṭṭīśvara built by Śāttaṉ Karuvūr alias Kannaradēva-Māyilaṭṭi a resident of the same place. This might be an inscription of the Koṅgu king Parakēsari Vikrama-Chōḷa whose inscription is also found in the same temple.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . kopparakecaripaṉmaṟku yāṇṭu Iraṇṭāvatu tuvaḷaic cāttaṉ
karuvūrāṉa kaṉṉarateva māyilaṭṭi Eṭuppitta nāṭṭīśvarattukkut tuvaḷai vaḷañciya
roma vaitta pokam devarkku śrīpali koṭṭu muvaccaṉ Araiya ṉaraiyaṉukkum makkaḷuk kum
vaitta jivitam Āṇṭuvarai nāṟkaḻañcum muṉṟokku nilamum maṉaiyun toṭṭamum vaiThe inscription stops here.
Digital edition of SII 19.28 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
